mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate indicates a strong labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE is below its 10-year average.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate drops significantly, it signals an accommodative monetary policy aimed at stimulating economic activity, which typically boosts equity valuations by lowering the cost of capital and increasing liquidity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy easing typically supports equity markets
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances following a triggered FEDFUNDS decline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust employment growth indicate healthy consumer demand and income, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively as investor sentiment improves.
failure_condition: If the interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS growth rates shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an acceleration in industrial production indicates increasing capacity utilization and business investment, signaling a strengthening economy that supports higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are negative in more than 35% of instances when INDPRO growth accelerates during a recovery phase.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer and business confidence, reduced investment, and potential future economic contraction, which negatively impacts corporate earnings expectations and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a triggered HOUST decline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and can lead to tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and reducing corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate revenues, and increased economic uncertainty, which drives S&P 500 forward returns negatively.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months AND PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 30% of instances following a combined significant deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating GDP growth indicates a robust and expanding economy, which underpins corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of periods following a triggered GDPC1 acceleration.
